methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $1.0B - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 23.4x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Substrate
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $1B+

data.narrative:
Black Semiconductor, a Series A chip design company founded by Daniel and Sebastian Schall, is evaluated against a comp universe of 3 public semiconductor leaders (ALAB, NVDA, AMD) and 8 private frontier-tech peers including Substrate, Cerebras, and Etched. The company has raised €254.4M to date.

The peer-median method is the primary applicable valuation approach. A set of 3 same-sector and same/adjacent-stage private comparables—Substrate (Series A, $1.0B), Positron AI (Series B, $1.0B), and Etched (Series B, $5.0B)—yields a median valuation of $1.0B with a range spanning $1.0B to $5.0B. Public-comp anchoring via price-to-sales multiples (median 23.4x across three public peers) cannot be applied, as Black Semiconductor's revenue is not disclosed; private companies at this stage rarely publish such figures.

The triangulated valuation range of $850M–$1.1B, centered on $1.0B, reflects the peer-median method alone, given data limitations. Key uncertainties include Black Semiconductor's actual revenue run rate and unit economics relative to the broader peer set, which would refine application of public-market multiples. The wide range observed in peer valuations ($1.0B–$5.0B) suggests meaningful dispersion in how the market prices similar-stage semiconductor ventures, leaving material room for upside or downside adjustment depending on Black Semiconductor's differentiation and growth trajectory.
